title: Roth, Bond, and Medicare Assumption Updates
subtitle: Message from Ron
intro: This update adds clearer Roth conversion sourcing, taxable bond balance buckets, and more conservative Medicare inflation modeling so Part B and IRMAA costs are not understated.

items.title: Choose the Roth conversion source
items.body: Use Higher IRA, Lower IRA, either named person first, or Percent split. A 50/50 split is supported, and the other IRA can finish the target if one side runs short.

items.title: Enter tax-free bonds by balance
items.body: Opening Year and Annual Check-In now break taxable-account balances into regular taxable investments, U.S. Treasuries, CA municipal bonds, and other-state municipal bonds. Enter balances, not interest.

items.title: Medicare costs are split from IRMAA thresholds
items.body: The model now separates IRMAA MAGI Threshold Inflation from Medicare Cost Inflation. This was added because Medicare Part B premiums and IRMAA surcharge dollars may rise faster than the MAGI income thresholds used to decide who pays IRMAA.

items.title: Use a strategy for future inflation changes
items.body: Strategy Builder now has Inflation Assumption Change. Use it for year ranges such as 2026-2035 Medicare Cost Inflation at 6.5%, then 2036+ at a lower rate. Leave any field blank to inherit the global Tax Tables setting.

items.title: Where to review assumptions
items.body: Use Setup Guide → Medicare & IRMAA for the plain-English controls. Use Tax Tables for expert edits to exact brackets, IRMAA thresholds, surcharge amounts, and standard Part B premiums.
